summary: Checksum-bound two-repository cass-memory seed and live cm-context retrieval evidence for the four retrospective anti-patterns.

acceptance.claim: cm context surfaces all four required anti-patterns in oraclemcp.
acceptance.evidence: cm context 'swarm quota gates publish persist self-fulfilling fixtures' --json returned success=true and all four b-omcp-e6 IDs in antiPatterns.

acceptance.claim: cm context surfaces the mirrored anti-patterns in rust-oracledb.
acceptance.evidence: The same command from the driver repo returned success=true and all four b-rodb-e6 IDs in antiPatterns.

limitations: The seeded memories are candidate/draft procedural rules with no helpful/harmful feedback history yet.
Session search history snippets returned by cm are not copied into this artifact; only the committed rule IDs and secret-safe acceptance result are recorded.
